Params: {1: 'Being behind the Gateway, <phonetic=I>you</phonetic> may now begin to put forward your hypothesis.', 3: '50'}
{2: 'Being behind the Gateway, <phonetic=I>you</phonetic> may now begin to put forward your hypothesis.'}
{1: 'Do you still remember where your "point of view" has come to rest?', 3: '50'}
{2: 'Do you still remember where your "point of view" has come to rest?'}
{1: 'Is it fixed on Alpha, swallowing all pain?', 3: '50'}
{2: 'Is it fixed on Alpha, swallowing all pain?'}
{1: 'On Lucia, as the Omega Core shatters?', 3: '50'}
{2: 'On Lucia, as the Omega Core shatters?'}
{1: 'On the F.O.S. starship, where every last light of the vanguard team has been extinguished?', 3: '50'}
{2: 'On the F.O.S. starship, where every last light of the vanguard team has been extinguished?'}
{1: 'Or has it settled, perhaps, on the <phonetic=yourself>human</phonetic> who already took the final breath in Alpha\'s arms?', 3: '50'}
{2: 'Or has it settled, perhaps, on the <phonetic=yourself>human</phonetic> who already took the final breath in Alpha\'s arms?'}
{1: 'It is as though your own life has become the most disposable thing you possess, the cheapest currency,\nreadily traded away for whatever it is you seek.', 3: '50'}
{2: 'It is as though your own life has become the most disposable thing you possess, the cheapest currency, readily traded away for whatever it is you seek.'}
{1: 'The hero finds a raw, primal joy in their own destruction.', 3: '50'}
{2: 'The hero finds a raw, primal joy in their own destruction.'}
{1: 'Fortunately, someone paid a far greater price, enough to return every point of view to you.', 3: '50'}
{2: 'Fortunately, someone paid a far greater price, enough to return every point of view to you.'}
{1: 'Now then, put forward your hypothesis. Where will you set your point of view? Where does your life begin?', 3: '50'}
{2: 'Now then, put forward your hypothesis. Where will you set your point of view? Where does your life begin?'}
{2: 'Keep it normal. Start from birth.', 3: '47', 4: 'Skip to the best part!', 5: '68'}
{1: 'You are born. A woman cradles you in her arms\nbefore the swaddling cloth can even be wrapped around you. The vernix seals your newborn eyes.', 3: '50'}
{2: 'You are born. A woman cradles you in her arms before the swaddling cloth can even be wrapped around you. The vernix seals your newborn eyes.'}
{1: 'The first words that reach you are feeble, yet brimming with hope.', 3: '50'}
{2: 'The first words that reach you are feeble, yet brimming with hope.'}
{1: '"You are my child, my precious darling."', 3: '50'}
{2: '"You are my child, my precious darling."'}

{1: '<s=#ffffffff>You merge with the Hetero█▃█▂ in the ocean\'s ▃■▇▂▄█, fusing with ■▄relik, Cthylla, Noan, ▃▄halt, and even ▂▇mia. This corpse ■▇▂ in ▄█ without end, choosing to parade on through ██.</s>', 3: '50'}

{2: '<s=#ffffffff>You merge with the Hetero█▃█▂ in the ocean\'s ▃■▇▂▄█, fusing with ■▄relik, Cthylla, Noan, ▃▄halt, and even ▂▇mia. This corpse ■▇▂ in ▄█ without end, choosing to parade on through ██.</s>'}

{1: '<s=#ffffffff>You are entombed within ▇▃▂▄ human ■█▃civilization that perishes thirty years from now. The power of ▂█ has drained from you; you are suspended in the amber of a dead world. Yet you are content to become a preserved slice of ▇▃█▂humanity,\nfor at last you ▄▃▂share a █■grave with the companions of your past days.</s>', 3: '50'}

{2: '<s=#ffffffff>You are entombed within ▇▃▂▄ human ■█▃civilization that perishes thirty years from now. The power of ▂█ has drained from you; you are suspended in the amber of a dead world. Yet you are content to become a preserved slice of ▇▃█▂humanity, for at last you ▄▃▂share a █■grave with the companions of your past days.</s>'}

{1: '<s=#ff4e4eff>You meet Lu■▇a at ▄▃▂ College. You fight as one, and your bond deepens with a quiet ▂▄■.\nWithout effort, you become those who know each ▇▃█ best,\nholding one another among the most ▃▃█▃ souls in your lives.</s>', 3: '50', 4: 'ff4e4eff'}

{2: '<s=#ff4e4eff><color=#ff4e4eff>You meet Lu■▇a at ▄▃▂ College. You fight as one, and your bond deepens with a quiet ▂▄■. Without effort, you become those who know each ▇▃█ best, holding one another among the most ▃▃█▃ souls in your lives.</color></s>'}
{1: 'You graduate from F.O.S. Military Command College. After training, you are appointed Commandant of the Gray Raven squad.\nUnder your command are three Constructs: Lucia, Liv, and Lee.', 3: '50'}
{2: 'You graduate from F.O.S. Military Command College. After training, you are appointed Commandant of the Gray Raven squad. Under your command are three Constructs: Lucia, Liv, and Lee.'}
{1: 'More than one person has spoken these words to you:', 3: '50'}
{2: 'More than one person has spoken these words to you:'}
{1: 'Would you make a choice? The path as it stands now is the one decided by [██].', 3: '50'}
{2: 'Would you make a choice? The path as it stands now is the one decided by [██].'}
{3: 'A human heart is made of flesh. When faced with such a hypothesis, it begins to ache.'}
{2: 'I want to go back. That\'s very important to me.', 3: '113'}
{2: 'There\'s someone I should be waiting for.', 3: '114'}
{1: '1', 3: 'The clamor of young voices gathering rises around you. The first figure to swim into view is a girl with silver-white hair.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'She catches another girl her age with swift, sure hands, but her earphones slip free and clatter to the ground.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'The silver-haired girl watches her leave, then touches her right ear. She hesitates for a beat, and starts scanning her surroundings.'}
{2: 'Lucia', 3: '...Thank you. Are you one of the first class too?', 4: '1'}
{3: '...The world goes quiet for half a second, long enough for you to hear, with perfect clarity, the pounding of your own heart.'}
{3: 'Your eyes blink, drinking in every detail of how Lucia looks now.'}
{3: 'Your mouth slowly opens and at last speaks those words to her.'}
{3: 'Before ever laying eyes on that cadet\'s name tag.'}
{3: 'Before the punishment of five hundred push-ups...'}
{3: 'Before the rotational training at the Airborne Command Post, before flying the aircraft past F.O.S...'}
{3: 'Before the birthday celebration on the grassy field at night...'}
{3: 'Before seeing the Earth rise from the Moon, just for the two of you...'}
{3: 'Before all of it, before everything, perhaps the only thing you could truly grasp was that first "I like you" spoken from behind Lucia\'s visor.'}
{3: 'But now, what has become unmistakable is the line that followed.'}
{3: 'The words are trapped behind the visor, heard by no one but yourself.'}
{3: 'Before a vow meant for Lucia was made ready, there in the F.O.S. auditorium...'}
{1: '1', 3: 'As Joanne turns every gaze toward this corner, two figures rise as one as if on cue.'}
{2: 'Lucia, there\'s something I want to tell you.', 3: '211'}
{1: '1', 3: 'Alpha steps through the Gateway. The imagined drop beneath her feet never comes.'}
{3: 'Instead, her foot meets solid ground, and a wave of disorientation so violent it nearly tears consciousness from her grasp.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'She lifts her gaze. The magnificent dome of F.O.S. College arches overhead.'}

{3: 'She then looks down. She is dressed in formal wear, the very outfit tailored for her graduation. She remembers, too, how she once took it out again, deliberately, for a moment of utmost importance.'}

{3: 'A tremor runs through her as if she needs to confirm something. With shaking hands, she gropes through her pocket until her fingers close around something smooth, round, warmed by her own heat...'}
{3: 'Round as a closed circle. The final link in the loop.', 4: '15'}
{3: 'In an instant, she feels it: the heartbeat she once had as a human. Every feeling she has ever known surges back into this body.'}
{1: 'And she remembers, with piercing clarity, what a happy day this had been.', 3: '50'}
{2: 'And she remembers, with piercing clarity, what a happy day this had been.'}
{3: 'And the day that demands the greatest courage to return to.'}
{3: 'Sound returns first. The irrepressible laughter and chatter of friends and alumni rush into the ears of the human named <phonetic=Alpha>Lucia</phonetic>.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'Then sight. She scans the room, taking in face after face, flushed bright with excitement, all of them achingly familiar.'}
{3: 'Ophelia. Adelyde. Cassandra... And Joanne, hands clamped over her mouth as if to hold back a scream.'}
{3: 'Everything seems to move in slow motion.'}
{3: 'The universe showed no mercy for that unfulfilled promise, yet someone who cared enough pulled her back into this moment.'}
{3: 'From behind her comes the sound of steady footsteps.'}
{2: '...There\'s something I want to tell you.', 3: '294'}
{3: 'The ring pressed tight in her palm, she turns back—slowly, so slowly—to look once more at the one standing before her.'}
{3: 'For a breath, she wants nothing more than to run away. But this choice is her last. And it matters most.'}
{3: 'So she raises her gaze and looks the person in the eye.'}
{3: 'She stumbles over the familiar line, forcing them out.'}
{3: 'But the <phonetic=lamb>human</phonetic> before her stays silent and simply offers a smile.'}
{3: 'Because in the human\'s gaze, she finds no trace of nervousness. No worry. No expectation.'}
{3: 'Those eyes still hold countless unspoken things, but all the youthful emotions have faded. What remains is a quiet, knowing happiness.'}
{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'But... it\'s no longer the same... as back then...', 4: '1'}
{3: 'She doesn\'t even notice the tremor in her own voice.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'She looks around, swift and searching, and only then notices that every face carries something subtly different from before.'}
{3: 'They are all smiling too. Smiles of quiet release.'}
{3: 'Even Joanne meets her eyes, offering a small nod of understanding.'}
{2: 'If you\'re going to stand by your choice,', 3: '334'}
{2: 'then you have to let this place go.', 3: '335'}
{2: 'I\'m with you.', 3: '338', 4: 'I don\'t support this, not one bit. But I...', 5: '338'}
{3: 'The human takes a half-step forward, and it sets off a small stir among everyone watching.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Joanne', 3: 'Oh, it\'s happening, it\'s happening... Who\'s going first with the ring?', 4: '18'}
{3: 'She draws her gaze back, no longer glancing around. There\'s no need for it anymore.'}
{3: 'The one before her begins to speak the words that were meant to be hers.'}
{3: 'She swallows down the tightness in her throat and goes on beneath the gaze of hundreds of watching eyes.'}
{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'We both remember. I shut off the comms first, then... I said something to you. Even now, I still...', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Probably just Earth\'s reflection on the visor.', 3: '361'}
{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'I told you back then, I\'d only say it once. I lied.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'There were so many times I wanted to say it again.', 4: '1'}

{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'Lying awake at three in the morning in North Africa, I wanted to say it. Checking my gear before a mission, I wanted to say it. And when I saw those sunset photos you sent from the South Pacific... so terribly shot, honestly. But I still wanted to say it.', 4: '1'}

{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'Riding back to the monastery, when I found you on the road and pulled you onto my bike. At the coating shop during the festival. At that flea market in Constellia, picking out a bed for the cat.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'Every single time... I wanted to say it.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Slowly, she draws her hand out from behind her back.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'Once again, she is the first to bring out the ring, and the human follows without hesitation.', 4: '15'}
{3: 'Two hands rise to the same height, each one holding a ring.', 4: '15'}
{3: 'They stand facing one another, eyes meeting through a veil of tears.', 4: '15'}
{3: 'She adds a witness to her vow, for this promise is one she will never take back.'}
{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'I will... not spend my life with you.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Right on cue, a thunderous crack splits the air above. The dome fractures, and the last world begins its collapse.'}
{2: 'Alpha', 3: 'I will not stay by your side forever.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'The words of the vow are agony, splintering her more completely than the rupturing dome, and she barely stops herself from taking it all back.'}
{2: 'Alpha', 3: '(But you are the one who matters most to me. You always have been.)', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Yet she leaves no room for retreat. She says nothing more.'}
{3: 'Still, the human is as understanding and forgiving as always, and that gives her the courage to say what she needs to say last.'}
{3: 'A cascade of splintered glass descends in fragments, each shard refracting the glow above and carrying within it the image of two faces, side by side.'}
